nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">More and more projects today are looking for transparent and reliable ways to collaborate with authors on X. This is exactly why <strong class="nn hq">Rally</strong> was created — a protocol designed to simplify the process of rewarding genuine, verifiable publications. It ensures that projects can trust their funds are not wasted but spent on proven contributions. The logic of the system is built to minimize manipulation and manual arrangements — saving both budget and effort. All actions are recorded on-chain, without intermediaries and with clear, transparent rules.</p><p id="0589" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">On the homepage, this is stated very clearly: anyone with an X account and their own audience can participate and earn, while verification and payouts happen automatically through <strong class="nn hq">GenLayer</strong> <strong class="nn hq">smart contracts</strong>. The workflow is also described: you join a campaign, complete a mission on X, the system evaluates the result, and transfers the reward. Below is an overview based entirely on <strong class="nn hq">Rally’s official materials</strong>.</p><h2 id="dab7" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">What is Rally</h2><p id="935d" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">Rally is an on-chain marketing protocol for X. Projects launch campaigns with missions, and authors participate through tweets, threads, and replies. Content and engagement are then verified using several AI models and strictly defined rules. Rewards are distributed automatically by smart contracts. The core idea is highly practical: pay for <strong class="nn hq">proven contributions</strong>, not for promises or flashy price tags. The website states clearly that the protocol is open to anyone with a voice and a community. Participation is free — all you need is an X account and a wallet to receive rewards.</p><figure class="ok ol om on oo nf mx my paragraph-image"><div role="button" tabindex="0" class="ng nh fw ni bd nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">On August 26, 2025, Rally’s blog published an introductory article where the team explained why the classic model of closed deals, informal agreements in chats, and reliance on superficial metrics like follower counts does not work. Rally offers an alternative: <strong class="nn hq">programmable participation rules, transparent quality assessment, and on-chain settlements</strong>. The article also notes that at the end of each period, campaign scores are aggregated into a unit of account called <strong class="nn hq">Rally Points</strong>, which serve as the basis for payouts according to the campaign’s rules.</p><h2 id="65ba" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">Who is behind this</h2><p id="fa29" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">The service is provided by Rally Foundation. On all pages, this organization is listed as the site owner and the one setting the terms. In the documentation, the combination of Rally and GenLayer is repeatedly mentioned. The first part (Rally) is responsible for the application, interface, EVM contracts, and distribution of funds in campaigns. The second part consists of GenLayer smart contracts, which verify that content complies with the rules, assess quality</p><p id="1b0f" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">and engagement, and help reach consensus on evaluations. In the documentation, this is described as <strong class="nn hq">“built on GenLayer.”</strong></p><p id="1ddc" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg"><strong class="nn hq"><em class="pt">“</em></strong><em class="pt">Multi-LLM Consensus: Submissions are verified by multi-LLM consensus on</em><strong class="nn hq"><em class="pt"> GenLayer”</em></strong></p><h2 id="9679" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">How Rally works inside</h2><p id="bc4d" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">The website has a short section that outlines the flow of actions. There are no applications or manual moderation. First, a campaign is selected, then a post is published on X, after which <strong class="nn hq">GenLayer smart contracts</strong> check whether the author has met the conditions. If so, the payment is sent on-chain. If you’re in a restricted territory, you cannot use the service.</p><p id="687f" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">Rally <strong class="nn hq">does not custody your funds or transact on your behalf</strong>. All transactions occur on the relevant network, <strong class="nn hq">are irreversible</strong>, and <strong class="nn hq">you connect your own wallet</strong> — so responsibility lies with you. The platform may charge <strong class="nn hq">service fees</strong>, and <strong class="nn hq">network fees</strong> are also on your side. Issues at the blockchain/network level are part of the <strong class="nn hq">user-accepted risk</strong>.</p><p id="fefb" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">Another important point concerns Rally’s role between projects and authors. Unless Rally itself is the campaign owner, it <strong class="nn hq">is not a party to your private terms</strong>. Compliance with specific requirements and advertising rules (including disclosure guidelines) rests with the <strong class="nn hq">campaign parties</strong> — consistent with a neutral protocol rather than an agency.</p><h2 id="65f3" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">Why It Matters for Authors</h2><p id="abb9" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">Rally values not just audience size but <strong class="nn hq">substance and real interaction</strong>. The FAQ highlights <strong class="nn hq">fair scoring for smaller accounts</strong> with genuine engagement. In plain terms, a smart thread with relevant reader reactions can beat a hollow promo post with a hundred thousand silent followers. For creators, this is a <strong class="nn hq">predictable way to earn from your voice and expertise</strong> — without waiting for closed lists.</p><p id="f1eb" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">In practice, a simple discipline helps: read the <strong class="nn hq">campaign brief</strong> before you tweet (it shows sources and what the system looks at). Keep a <strong class="nn hq">dialogue in replies</strong> — both reply quality and the <strong class="nn hq">audience of repliers</strong> matter. If a campaign runs in periods, <strong class="nn hq">refresh engagement</strong> so only growth is counted. And don’t skimp on meaning: context-free reposts contribute little to quality, which is the <strong class="nn hq">foundation of final distribution</strong>.</p><h2 id="80a9" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">Why It Matters for Projects</h2><p id="8cd2" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">Teams hate paying for noise. Rally makes it <strong class="nn hq">predictable</strong>: you set <strong class="nn hq">metric weights</strong> and choose a <strong class="nn hq">distribution curve</strong>. Want a fast sprint that favors top performers? Use a more <strong class="nn hq">concentrated curve</strong>. Need broad community education? Choose a more <strong class="nn hq">even profile</strong>. Payouts follow clear rules, and <strong class="nn hq">on-chain attribution</strong> simplifies reporting and internal analysis.</p><p id="e554" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">There’s also an <strong class="nn hq">Alpha Partner Program</strong>: zero fees during alpha, token matching, promo priority, and the chance to influence the roadmap. For new protocols, it’s a way to arrive early and <strong class="nn hq">set campaign standards</strong> alongside the Rally team.</p><h2 id="6526" class="op oq hp bb or os ot ou ov ow ox oy oz pa pb pc pd pe pf pg ph pi pj pk pl pm bg">Short Roadmap & Current Stage</h2><p id="6b73" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no pn nq nr ns po nu nv nw pp ny nz oa pq oc od oe pr og oh oi hi bg">Rally is in <strong class="nn hq">private alpha</strong>: access opens in waves for active waitlist users, per priorities explained on the blog. After a few weeks, the plan is to open <strong class="nn hq">beta</strong>. This phase includes <strong class="nn hq">access points</strong> (score) and community activities.</p><p id="6ae3" class="pw-post-body-paragraph nl nm hp nn b no np nq nr ns nt nu nv nw nx ny nz oa ob oc od oe of og oh oi hi bg">Rally rewards <strong class="nn hq">real contribution, not loudness</strong>. Authors write with purpose and engage; projects define clear rules and honest priorities. The protocol handles the rest. If you’ve wanted marketing <strong class="nn hq">without back-room deals</strong>, this is where <strong class="nn hq">rules live in code</strong> and <strong class="nn hq">settlement lives
